name: Web - Login Brute Force Attack
description: Detects brute force login attacks against web applications by monitoring POST requests to common login endpoints. Triggers when a single IP sends many POST requests resulting in 401/403 responses within a short window.
search: index=web (sourcetype=nginx_access OR sourcetype=apache_access) method=POST (status=401 OR status=403 OR status=200) | eval is_login_endpoint=if(match(lower(uri), "(login|signin|auth|session|account|wp-login|admin|user|password|token)"), 1, 0) | where is_login_endpoint=1 | bucket _time span=5m | stats count as req_count, dc(uri) as endpoints, values(status) as status_codes, values(uri) as login_uris by src_ip, host, _time | where req_count >= 20 | eval failure_indicator=if(like(mvjoin(status_codes, ","), "%401%") OR like(mvjoin(status_codes, ","), "%403%"), "yes", "no") | eval severity="high" | eval mitre="T1110.001" | sort -req_count | table _time, src_ip, host, req_count, endpoints, login_uris, status_codes, failure_indicator, severity, mitre
author: SOC Team
